help.modal.title: ENDIANNESS GUIDE
help.intro:
WHAT IS ENDIANNESS?

Endianness defines the byte order in which a multi-byte value is stored in memory. The central question is: which byte goes to the lowest address?

The two main formats are Little Endian and Big Endian.

💡 The name comes from "Gulliver's Travels" — the war between those who break eggs from the small end (little-endians) and those who break from the big end (big-endians).

x86/x64 CPUs (Intel, AMD) → Little Endian
TCP/IP Protocols → Big Endian ("network byte order")
ARM → bi-endian (configurable)
help.little:
LITTLE ENDIAN

The least significant byte (LSB) goes to the lowest address in memory.

Example: value 0xDEADBEEF starting at 0x0000:

+0
+1
+2
+3
0x0000
EF
BE
AD
DE

EF (LSB) → 0x0000  |  DE (MSB) → 0x0003

✓ Used by: x86, x64, ARM (default), RISC-V
✓ Advantage: integer extension without changing the base address
help.big:
BIG ENDIAN

The most significant byte (MSB) goes to the lowest address in memory.

Example: value 0xDEADBEEF starting at 0x0000:

+0
+1
+2
+3
0x0000
DE
AD
BE
EF

DE (MSB) → 0x0000  |  EF (LSB) → 0x0003

✓ Used by: SPARC, PowerPC, TCP/IP
✓ In the simulator: comparative/visual mode; real Intel execution remains little-endian
help.ops:
OPERATIONS

STORE — Writes the register value to memory, byte by byte. The nibble being sent is highlighted and an animated packet flies to the memory cell.

LOAD — Reads bytes from memory into the register. The register starts at zero and is filled byte by byte in real time — you see the value being built!

STEP — Executes the full FETCH → DECODE → EXECUTE cycle, updating PC, registers, memory and stack per the instruction.

CLEAR — Resets memory and registers.

💡 Click any memory cell to select that address.
💡 Use DWORD to see the full Little vs Big Endian effect.
💡 Slow down to follow each byte carefully.
💡 Save 💾 and load 📂 simulations to share.
log.sys.stack_size: Stack size adjusted to {0}. {1} reset at 0x{2}. Memory map now covers 0x0000..0x{3}.
log.sys.demo_loaded: Simulator started with {0} demo program: main + 2 functions using the stack.
log.sys.demo_arch: {0} demo program loaded at 0x0000: main + 2 functions with stack usage.
log.sys.nop_fill: Remaining bytes at 0x{0}..0x{1} filled with NOP.
log.sys.step_start: STEP at 0x{0} — full cycle of the current instruction.
log.info.fetch1: FETCH  IP=0x{0} | opcode=0x{1} | {2} byte(s) · Intel SDM Vol.1 §6.3
log.info.fetch2: FETCH  IP ← 0x{0}  (incremented during fetch, Intel SDM Vol.1 §6.3)
log.info.fetch_desc: Fetches {0} byte(s) at 0x{1} and loads the instruction into the instruction register.
log.info.fetch_ip: {0} advances to 0x{1} after FETCH, before the instruction takes effect.
log.info.decode_desc: The fetched bytes are decoded as {0}.
log.info.execute_desc: Applies the architectural effects of the decoded instruction on PC, registers, memory and stack as needed.
log.error.asm_grew: Instruction at 0x{0} grew from {1}B to {2}B and overwrote the following flow.
log.push: PUSH {0}=0x{1}: {2} decrements to 0x{3} and the data goes to the stack top [0x{3}].
log.pop: POP [0x{0}] → {1}: data leaves stack top and {2} will be incremented after the read.
